titleStmt.title: SII 26.469: original edition by P. R. Srinivasan
No. 469. (A.R. No. 446 of 1909.) MAṆAPPAḌAIVĪḌU, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.

summary.p: North wall of the same maṇḍapa.

p.hi: Tribhº Sundarapāṇḍyadēva II: Year 10, Viṛiśchika (Kārttigai) śu. 8, Rēvatī = 1248, A.D., November 25, The nakshatra was Pūraṭṭādi.

summary.p: This inscription records a gift of 150 paṇam by a certain Tirunāvuḍaiyār of Ambalattāḍinallūr alias Maṇappaiḍaivīḍu in Kīḻkaḷak-kūṟṟam to provide for food and other offerings to the images of the god, goddess, and Iḷaiya-nāyaṉār (i.e., Subrahmaṇya ?) set up by him in the name of Sundarapāṇḍya Kaṇḍiyadēvar in the temple of the god Mūṉṟukaiyīśvaramuḍaiyanāyaṉār of the place. It is stated that the money was received by the Śivabrāhmaṇas of the 30 circles attached to the temple, who had agreed to carry out the object of the endowment.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
§ svastiśrī . komāṟapaṉmarāṉa tribhuvaṉaccakravattikaḷ Emmaṇṭalamuṅ koṇṭaruḷiya śrīsundarapāṇṭiya tevaṟku yāṇṭu 10 vatu praścikanāyaṟṟu pūrvvapakṣattu Aṣṭamiyum peṟṟa revatināḷ kiḻakaḷakkūṟṟattu Ampalattāṭinallūrāṉa maṇappaṭaiviṭṭu Uṭaiyār muṉṟakaiśvaramuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyilil muppatu vaṭṭattu śivabrāhmaṇarom Ivūr U
ṭaiyāṉ tirunāvuṭaiyāṟku Ubhayapramāṇam paṇṇik kuṭutta paricāvatu Iṉṉāyiṉār koyilukku Ivar cuntarapāṇṭiyak kaṇṭiyatevar perāl Eḻuntaruḷap paṇṇiṉa nāyakarum nācciyārum Iḷaiyanāyaṉāṟkum Amutupaṭi cāttuppaṭi Uḷaviṭṭa nitta nimantattukku niccayitta Amutupaṭi Aruci Aṟunāḻiyum kaṟiyamutu veñceṉattukkum ma
ṟṟuḷḷaṉavaiyiṟṟukkum Ivar pakkal nāṅkaḷ Ubhayattukkup paṟṟiṉa ø100 5 10 Ippaṇam nūṟṟu Aimpatukkum Iṉṉāyiṉāṟkku Ippaṭi Uḷḷa Amutupaṭi cāttuppaṭi Uḷḷa nittanimantam muṭṭāmal cantirātittavaṟc cella kallilum cempilum veṭṭikkoḷvatāka Ippaṭi cammatittu Ubhayapramāṇam paṇṇik kuṭuttom muppa
tuvaṭṭattu civabrāhmaṇarom Uṭaiyāṉ ItirunāvuṭaiyāṟkuIncomplete.
Digital edition of SII 26.469 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
